canonical_definition: Based on the provided sources, ToolAlpaca is a dataset designed for tool learning in large language models. It is described as an "agent-generated tool learning dataset" composed of 495 tools and 3975 training examples. The data indicates its use for both training and evaluation of tool-augmented LLMs, specifically within "controlled settings." As a measurement instrument, ToolAlpaca is used to assess the behavior of "Tool use" and is also linked to the broader concept of "Evaluation." One source provides a concrete example of its application in training, noting that a model named "ToolAlpaca 7B" is fine-tuned on this dataset.
papers: Can Compressed LLMs Truly Act? An Empirical Evaluation of Agentic Capabilities in LLM Compression
Prune ’n Predict: Optimizing LLM Decision-making with Conformal Prediction

definitions.text: A tool learning dataset with 495 tools and 3975 training examples, used to train and evaluate tool-augmented LLMs in controlled settings.

relationships.paper: PROMPTEVALS: A Dataset of Assertions and Guardrails for Custom Production Large Language Model Pipelines
